questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: She _____ to school every day.
questions.solution: 主语为第三人称单数，一般现在时动词加 -s/-es。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: They _____ football at the moment.
questions.solution: at the moment 表示现在正在进行的动作，用现在进行时。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: He _____ his homework yesterday.
questions.solution: yesterday 表示过去时间，用一般过去时。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: I _____ to Beijing next week.
questions.solution: next week 表示将来时间，用一般将来时 will + 动词原形。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: She _____ English for five years.
questions.solution: for five years 表示从过去开始持续到现在的动作，用现在完成进行时。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: By the time he arrived, we _____ already _____ the meeting.
questions.solution: by the time + 过去时间，主句用过去完成时。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: The train _____ at 6 p.m. every day.
questions.solution: 表示按时刻表发生的动作，用一般现在时。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: When I saw her, she _____ a book.
questions.solution: 表示过去某一时刻正在进行的动作，用过去进行时。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: I _____ my keys. I can't find them anywhere.
questions.solution: 现在完成时表示过去动作对现在的影响（钥匙丢了，现在找不到）。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: By next year, they _____ in this city for 20 years.
questions.solution: by next year + for 20 years 表示到将来某时持续的动作，用将来完成进行时。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: While I _____, the phone rang.
questions.solution: while 引导的从句表示过去正在进行的动作，用过去进行时。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: She _____ never _____ to Japan before.
questions.solution: before 与过去完成时连用，表示在过去某个时间之前未曾去过。

questions.title: 时态选择
questions.content: Look! The children _____ in the garden.
questions.solution: Look! 暗示正在发生的动作，用现在进行时。

questions.title: 时态选择

questions.solution: since 引导的时间状语从句，主句用现在完成时。

questions.solution: 主句为过去时(said)，宾语从句用过去将来时(would)。

questions.solution: for three hours 表示持续到现在，用现在完成进行时。

questions.title: 语态转换
questions.content:
Change the following sentence to active voice:

"The road is being repaired."
questions.solution:
被动语态转主动语态：找出动作的执行者作主语，动词变为主动形式。
正确答案：They are repairing the road.
options: A. People must obey the law.
C. They have completed the project.
D. They are repairing the road.

"A new bridge will be built."
questions.solution:
被动语态转主动语态：找出动作的执行者作主语，动词变为主动形式。
正确答案：They will build a new bridge.
options: A. They will build a new bridge.
B. They will announce the results tomorrow.
C. The storm destroyed the house.
D. They have completed the project.

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: If I _____ you, I would accept the offer.
questions.solution: 与现在事实相反的虚拟语气，be 动词一律用 were。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: I wish I _____ to the party last night.
questions.solution: wish 后的宾语从句，与过去事实相反用过去完成时 had gone。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: If it _____ tomorrow, we would cancel the picnic.
questions.solution: 与将来事实可能相反的虚拟语气，用 should + 动词原形。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: She speaks as if she _____ everything.
questions.solution: as if 引导的从句，表示与现在事实相反的假设，用一般过去时。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: If he had studied harder, he _____ the exam.
questions.solution: 与过去事实相反的虚拟语气，主句用 would have + 过去分词。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: It is essential that every student _____ the rules.
questions.solution: It is essential that... 句型中，从句用虚拟语气 (should) + 动词原形。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: I would rather you _____ here tomorrow.
questions.solution: would rather 后的从句用一般过去时表示将来。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: If only I _____ the truth earlier!
questions.solution: if only 与过去事实相反，用过去完成时。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: It's high time that we _____ action.
questions.solution: It's (high) time that... 句型中，从句用一般过去时。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: The doctor suggested that he _____ more exercise.
questions.solution: suggest 表示「建议」时，宾语从句用 (should) + 动词原形。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: Without your help, I _____ the task.
questions.solution: without 短语表示与过去事实相反的假设，主句用 would have + 过去分词。

questions.title: 虚拟语气

questions.solution: 与现在事实相反的虚拟语气，be 动词用 were。

questions.title: 虚拟语气

questions.solution: demand 后的宾语从句用 (should) + 动词原形。

questions.title: 虚拟语气
questions.content: I wish it _____ raining. I want to go out.
questions.solution: wish 表示对将来的愿望，用 would + 动词原形。

questions.title: 虚拟语气

questions.solution: 与过去事实相反的虚拟语气，if 从句用过去完成时。

questions.title: 定语从句
questions.content: I still remember the day _____ we first met.
questions.solution: 先行词为时间，关系副词用 when。

questions.title: 定语从句
questions.content: The girl _____ mother is a doctor is my classmate.
questions.solution: 表示所属关系，用 whose。

questions.title: 定语从句
questions.content: Everything _____ he said was true.
questions.solution: 先行词为不定代词 everything，关系代词只能用 that。

questions.title: 定语从句
questions.content: The reason _____ he was late is unknown.
questions.solution: 先行词为 reason，关系副词用 why。

questions.title: 定语从句
questions.content: He is the only person _____ can solve this problem.
questions.solution: 先行词被 the only 修饰，关系代词用 that。

questions.title: 定语从句
questions.content: The students, _____ had finished their homework, went home.
questions.solution: 非限制性定语从句，关系代词指人用 who。

questions.title: 定语从句
questions.content: Is this the factory _____ you visited last year?
questions.solution: 先行词在从句中作宾语（visited the factory），用 that/which。

questions.title: 定语从句填空
questions.content: I lost the pen _____ (that/who) you gave me.
questions.solution: 先行词 pen 指物，用 that/which。

questions.title: 定语从句填空
questions.content: The person _____ (who/which) helped me was very kind.
questions.solution: 先行词 person 指人，用 who。

questions.title: 定语从句填空
questions.content: This is the room _____ (where/which) the meeting was held.
questions.solution: room 表示地点，在从句中作状语，用 where。

questions.title: 名词性从句
questions.content: _____ he said surprised everyone.
questions.solution: what 引导主语从句，在从句中作 said 的宾语，表示「...的话」。

questions.title: 名词性从句
questions.content: The fact _____ she is married is not a secret.
questions.solution: that 引导同位语从句，解释 fact 的内容。

questions.title: 名词性从句
questions.content: I don't know _____ he will come or not.
questions.solution: whether 引导宾语从句，表示「是否」。

questions.title: 名词性从句
questions.content: The question is _____ we can finish the work on time.
questions.solution: whether 引导表语从句，表示「是否」。

questions.title: 名词性从句
questions.content: _____ wins the election is not important to me.
questions.solution: who 引导主语从句，在从句中作主语。

questions.title: 名词性从句
questions.content: It is known _____ smoking is harmful to health.
questions.solution: it 作形式主语，that 引导真正的主语从句。

questions.title: 名词性从句
questions.content: I have no idea _____ she is talking about.
questions.solution: what 引导同位语从句，在从句中作 about 的宾语。

questions.title: 名词性从句
questions.content: _____ we need is more time.
questions.solution: what 引导主语从句，在从句中作 need 的宾语。

questions.content: _____ (That/What) he said is true.
questions.solution: What 在从句中作 said 的宾语。

questions.content: _____ (That/What) he said is true.
questions.solution: What 在从句中作 said 的宾语。

questions.content: The news _____ (that/what) our team won excited everyone.
questions.solution: that 引导同位语从句，解释 news 的内容。

questions.title: 状语从句
questions.content: I will call you _____ I arrive.
questions.solution: when 引导时间状语从句，表示「当...的时候」。

questions.title: 状语从句
questions.content: She didn't go to work _____ she was ill.
questions.solution: because 引导原因状语从句，表示「因为」。

questions.title: 状语从句
questions.content: _____ it rains, the picnic will be canceled.
questions.solution: if 引导条件状语从句，表示「如果」。

questions.title: 状语从句
questions.content: He studies hard _____ he can get into a good university.
questions.solution: so that 引导目的状语从句，表示「为了」。

questions.title: 状语从句
questions.content: _____ he is young, he has a lot of experience.
questions.solution: although 引导让步状语从句，表示「虽然」。

questions.title: 状语从句
questions.content: He was _____ tired _____ he fell asleep immediately.
questions.solution: so...that... 引导结果状语从句，表示「如此...以至于...」。

questions.title: 状语从句
questions.content: You won't succeed _____ you work hard.
questions.solution: unless 引导条件状语从句，表示「除非...否则...」。

questions.title: 状语从句
questions.content: He has been working here _____ he graduated.
questions.solution: since 引导时间状语从句，表示「自从...以来」。

questions.title: 状语从句
questions.content: _____ the weather was bad, we still enjoyed the trip.
questions.solution: even though 引导让步状语从句，表示「即使...」。

questions.title: 状语从句
questions.content: _____ you have finished your homework, you can go out.
questions.solution: as long as 引导条件状语从句，表示「只要...」。

questions.title: 状语从句填空
questions.content: He spoke loudly _____ (so that/because) everyone could hear him.
questions.solution: so that 引导目的状语从句。

questions.title: 状语从句填空
questions.content: He spoke loudly _____ (so that/because) everyone could hear him.
questions.solution: so that 引导目的状语从句。

questions.title: 状语从句填空
questions.content: We left _____ (before/after) the party ended.
questions.solution: before 表示「在...之前」。

questions.title: 状语从句填空
questions.content: He spoke loudly _____ (so that/because) everyone could hear him.
questions.solution: so that 引导目的状语从句。

questions.title: 状语从句填空

questions.title: 倒装句
questions.content: Never _____ seen such a beautiful sunset.
questions.solution: 否定副词 Never 置于句首，句子用部分倒装（助动词/情态动词 + 主语 + 动词）。

questions.title: 倒装句
questions.content: Not until he arrived _____ the truth.
questions.solution: Not until 置于句首，主句用部分倒装。注意：此题 Not until 不在句首，不用倒装。

questions.title: 倒装句
questions.content: Only in this way _____ solve the problem.
questions.solution: only + 状语置于句首时，句子用部分倒装。

questions.title: 倒装句
questions.content: So fast _____ that I couldn't catch up with him.
questions.solution: so + 形容词/副词置于句首时，句子用部分倒装。

questions.title: 倒装句
questions.content: Hardly _____ the station when the train left.
questions.solution: hardly...when... 句型中，hardly 后的句子用过去完成时倒装。

questions.title: 倒装句
questions.content: Not only _____ English, but he also speaks French.
questions.solution: not only 置于句首，句子用部分倒装。

questions.title: 倒装句
questions.content: Little _____ that the project would be so difficult.
questions.solution: little 表示否定意义，置于句首时句子用部分倒装。

questions.title: 倒装句
questions.content: _____ the first day, I knew I would love this job.
questions.solution: scarcely...when... 句型，scarcely 后用过去完成时倒装。

questions.title: 倒装句
questions.content: Only when you have finished your homework _____ go out.
questions.solution: only + 状语从句置于句首，主句用部分倒装。

questions.title: 倒装句
questions.content: _____ the truth, I wouldn't have believed it.
questions.solution: 虚拟条件句省略 if，将 had 置于句首构成倒装。
options: B. If I had not seen

questions.title: The Rise of Artificial Intelligence
questions.content:
[Passage 1]

The Rise of Artificial Intelligence

Artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming the world in ways we never imagined possible. From self-driving cars to voice assistants like Siri and Alexa, AI has become an integral part of our daily lives. While AI offers tremendous benefits such as improved efficiency and accuracy in various fields, it also raises important ethical questions. For example, as AI systems become more capable of performing tasks that were once reserved for humans, there is growing concern about job displacement. Experts suggest that the key to addressing this challenge lies in education and retraining programs that help workers adapt to the changing job market.

---

What is the main topic of this passage?
questions.solution: 文章主要讨论了AI对社会的影响，包括其好处和挑战。
options: B. The impact of AI on society

questions.title: The Future of Space Exploration
questions.content:
[Passage 2]

The Future of Space Exploration

Space exploration has entered a new era. Private companies like SpaceX and Blue Origin are now competing with government agencies to push the boundaries of what is possible. The ultimate goal for many is to establish a human presence on Mars. Scientists believe that Mars, with its similarities to Earth, could potentially support human life. However, the challenges are enormous. Astronauts would need to survive extreme temperatures, radiation, and the psychological stress of long-term isolation. Despite these obstacles, the progress being made is remarkable, and many experts predict that a manned mission to Mars could happen within the next two decades.

---

What is the ultimate goal mentioned in the passage?
questions.solution: 文章明确指出 The ultimate goal for many is to establish a human presence on Mars。
options: B. Establishing a human presence on Mars

questions.title: The Benefits of Reading
questions.content:
[Passage 3]

The Benefits of Reading

Reading is one of the most beneficial activities for personal growth. Studies have shown that regular reading can improve vocabulary, enhance empathy, and reduce stress levels. When people read fiction, they are essentially putting themselves in someone else's shoes, which helps develop emotional intelligence and understanding of different perspectives. Moreover, reading before bedtime has been found to improve sleep quality, as it helps the mind relax and transition away from the blue light of electronic devices. In an age of constant digital distraction, the simple act of reading a book for 30 minutes a day can have profound effects on mental well-being and cognitive function.

---

What is the main benefit of reading mentioned in the passage?
questions.solution: 文章第一句说 Reading is one of the most beneficial activities for personal growth。

questions.title: Climate Change and Its Effects
questions.content:
[Passage 4]

Climate Change and Its Effects

Climate change is one of the most pressing issues of our time. The Earth's average temperature has risen by about 1 degree Celsius since the late 19th century, largely due to increased carbon dioxide emissions from human activities. This warming has led to more frequent extreme weather events, rising sea levels, and the loss of biodiversity. Scientists warn that if we do not take immediate action to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, the consequences could be catastrophic. However, there is hope. Renewable energy sources like solar and wind power are becoming more affordable, and many countries are committing to carbon neutrality goals. Individual actions, such as reducing waste and using public transportation, can also make a difference.

---

What is the main cause of climate change according to the passage?
questions.solution: 文章说 largely due to increased carbon dioxide emissions from human activities。

questions.title: The Importance of Education
questions.content:
[Passage 5]

The Importance of Education

Education is widely recognized as the foundation of personal and societal development. It provides individuals with the knowledge and skills needed to participate effectively in society and the economy. Beyond academic learning, education helps develop critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving abilities. Countries that invest heavily in education tend to have higher levels of innovation and economic growth. However, access to quality education remains unequal around the world. Many children in developing countries still lack basic educational opportunities. Closing this education gap is essential for achieving global equality and sustainable development.

---

What does education provide according to the passage?
questions.solution: 文章说 It provides individuals with the knowledge and skills needed to participate effectively in society and the economy。
options: B. Knowledge and skills for society

questions.title: The History of the Internet
questions.content:
[Passage 6]

The History of the Internet

The Internet has revolutionized the way we communicate, work, and access information. Its origins can be traced back to the 1960s when the United States Department of Defense developed ARPANET, a network designed to allow computers to communicate with each other. In the 1990s, the World Wide Web was created by Tim Berners-Lee, making the Internet accessible to the general public. Since then, the Internet has grown exponentially. Today, billions of people use the Internet for everything from social networking to online shopping and remote work. While the Internet has brought many benefits, it has also created challenges such as privacy concerns, cybercrime, and the spread of misinformation.

---

When did the origins of the Internet begin?
questions.solution: 文章说 Its origins can be traced back to the 1960s。

questions.title: Healthy Eating Habits
questions.content:
[Passage 7]

Healthy Eating Habits

Maintaining a healthy diet is essential for overall well-being. A balanced diet should include a variety of fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins. Nutritionists recommend limiting the intake of processed foods, sugar, and saturated fats, which have been linked to various health problems including obesity, heart disease, and diabetes. It is also important to stay hydrated by drinking plenty of water throughout the day. In addition to what we eat, how we eat matters too. Eating slowly and mindfully can help with digestion and prevent overeating. Making small, sustainable changes to eating habits is more effective than following extreme diets that are difficult to maintain.

---

What should a balanced diet include?
questions.solution: 文章说 A balanced diet should include a variety of fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins。
options: B. Fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins

questions.title: The Power of Teamwork
questions.content:
[Passage 8]

The Power of Teamwork

Teamwork is a fundamental aspect of success in both professional and personal settings. When people work together effectively, they can achieve more than any individual could alone. Good teamwork requires clear communication, mutual respect, and a shared vision. Each team member brings unique strengths and perspectives that contribute to the overall success of the group. However, teamwork also comes with challenges. Conflicts may arise when team members have different opinions or working styles. The key to overcoming these challenges is to maintain open communication and focus on the common goal rather than personal differences. Successful teams celebrate their achievements together and learn from their failures as a group.

---

What is required for good teamwork according to the passage?
questions.solution: 文章说 Good teamwork requires clear communication, mutual respect, and a shared vision。
options: B. Clear communication and mutual respect

questions.title: The Psychology of Happiness
questions.content:
[Passage 9]

The Psychology of Happiness

What makes people happy? This question has fascinated philosophers and scientists for centuries. Research in positive psychology suggests that happiness is not primarily determined by external circumstances such as wealth or status, but rather by internal factors like gratitude, meaningful relationships, and a sense of purpose. Studies show that people who regularly practice gratitude by keeping a journal or expressing thanks to others tend to be happier. Additionally, strong social connections are one of the strongest predictors of happiness. Engaging in activities that provide a sense of flow—where one is fully absorbed in a challenging but enjoyable task—also contributes significantly to well-being. Ultimately, happiness is less about what we have and more about how we think and connect with others.

---

What does research suggest about happiness?
questions.solution: 文章说 happiness is not primarily determined by external circumstances... but rather by internal factors。
options: A. It depends mainly on wealth
B. It is determined by internal factors

questions.title: The World of Social Media
questions.content:
[Passage 10]

The World of Social Media

Social media has become an inseparable part of modern life. Platforms like Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter connect billions of people across the globe. On the positive side, social media allows people to stay in touch with friends and family, share experiences, and access news and information instantly. It has also become a powerful tool for businesses to reach customers and for activists to organize movements. However, social media also has a darker side. Excessive use has been linked to increased anxiety, depression, and feelings of loneliness, especially among young people. The spread of fake news and online harassment are also serious concerns. Experts recommend using social media mindfully and taking regular breaks to maintain mental health.

---

What is a positive aspect of social media mentioned in the passage?
questions.solution: 文章说 social media allows people to stay in touch with friends and family。
options: B. Staying in touch with friends and family

questions.title: The Science of Sleep
questions.content:
[Passage 11]

The Science of Sleep

Sleep is essential for human health and well-being. During sleep, the body repairs itself and the brain processes information from the day, consolidating memories and clearing out toxins. Most adults need between 7 and 9 hours of sleep per night, yet many people get far less due to busy schedules, stress, or the use of electronic devices before bedtime. The blue light emitted by phones and computers can interfere with the body's natural sleep-wake cycle by suppressing the production of melatonin, a hormone that regulates sleep. To improve sleep quality, experts recommend establishing a regular sleep schedule, avoiding screens for at least an hour before bed, and creating a comfortable sleep environment that is cool, dark, and quiet.

---

How much sleep do most adults need?
questions.solution: 文章说 Most adults need between 7 and 9 hours of sleep per night。

questions.title: The Science of Sleep
questions.content:
[Passage 11]

The Science of Sleep

Sleep is essential for human health and well-being. During sleep, the body repairs itself and the brain processes information from the day, consolidating memories and clearing out toxins. Most adults need between 7 and 9 hours of sleep per night, yet many people get far less due to busy schedules, stress, or the use of electronic devices before bedtime. The blue light emitted by phones and computers can interfere with the body's natural sleep-wake cycle by suppressing the production of melatonin, a hormone that regulates sleep. To improve sleep quality, experts recommend establishing a regular sleep schedule, avoiding screens for at least an hour before bed, and creating a comfortable sleep environment that is cool, dark, and quiet.

---

How does blue light affect sleep?
questions.solution: 文章说 blue light... can interfere with the body's natural sleep-wake cycle by suppressing the production of melatonin。

questions.title: Cultural Diversity in the Modern World
questions.content:
[Passage 12]

Cultural Diversity in the Modern World

Cultural diversity refers to the variety of human societies and cultures in the world. In today's globalized world, people from different cultural backgrounds interact more frequently than ever before. This diversity enriches our lives by exposing us to different perspectives, traditions, cuisines, and forms of art. However, cultural differences can also lead to misunderstandings and conflicts if not approached with respect and openness. Learning about other cultures helps break down stereotypes and promotes mutual understanding. Schools and workplaces are increasingly recognizing the importance of cultural competence—the ability to interact effectively with people from different cultures. Embracing diversity is not just about tolerance; it is about celebrating the unique contributions that each culture brings to the global community.

---

What does cultural diversity refer to?
questions.solution: 文章第一句说 Cultural diversity refers to the variety of human societies and cultures。
options: B. The variety of human societies and cultures

questions.title: The Importance of Physical Exercise
questions.content:
[Passage 13]

The Importance of Physical Exercise

Regular physical exercise is one of the best things you can do for your health. It helps control weight, reduces the risk of heart disease, strengthens bones and muscles, and improves mental health and mood. The World Health Organization recommends that adults get at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity exercise per week. This can include activities like brisk walking, cycling, swimming, or dancing. Even small amounts of physical activity can make a difference. Taking the stairs instead of the elevator, walking during lunch breaks, or doing simple stretches at your desk can all contribute to better health. The key is to find activities that you enjoy, as you are more likely to stick with them over the long term.

---

How much exercise does the WHO recommend per week?
questions.solution: 文章说 The World Health Organization recommends that adults get at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity exercise per week。

questions.title: The Digital Age and Privacy
questions.content:
[Passage 14]

The Digital Age and Privacy

In the digital age, personal privacy has become a growing concern. Every time we use a smartphone, browse the internet, or make an online purchase, we leave behind a digital footprint. Companies collect vast amounts of data about our preferences, habits, and behaviors to target advertisements and improve their services. While this can lead to more personalized experiences, it also raises questions about how our data is being used and who has access to it. Data breaches and identity theft are real risks in today's connected world. To protect privacy, experts recommend using strong passwords, being cautious about what information you share online, and regularly reviewing privacy settings on social media and other platforms.

---

What is a digital footprint?
questions.solution: 文章说 we leave behind a digital footprint，指使用数字设备时留下的数据痕迹。
options: B. Data left behind when using digital devices

questions.title: The Value of Time Management
questions.content:
[Passage 15]

The Value of Time Management

Time management is a crucial skill for success in both academic and professional life. Effective time management allows individuals to accomplish more in less time, reduces stress, and creates opportunities for personal growth and leisure. One of the most popular time management techniques is the Pomodoro Technique, which involves working in focused 25-minute intervals followed by short breaks. Another useful strategy is prioritizing tasks using the Eisenhower Matrix, which categorizes tasks based on their urgency and importance. Planning the day in advance and setting realistic goals can also help people stay on track. However, it is important to remain flexible, as unexpected events can disrupt even the best-laid plans. The goal of time management is not to fill every minute with work, but to use time wisely to achieve a balanced life.

---

What is the Pomodoro Technique?
questions.solution: 文章说 the Pomodoro Technique, which involves working in focused 25-minute intervals followed by short breaks。
options: B. Working in 25-minute intervals with breaks

questions.title: The Beauty of Natural Wonders
questions.content:
[Passage 16]

The Beauty of Natural Wonders

Our planet is home to some truly breathtaking natural wonders. From the majestic Grand Canyon in the United States to the stunning Northern Lights in the Arctic, nature never fails to inspire awe. These natural formations are the result of millions of years of geological processes, weather patterns, and ecological evolution. Unfortunately, many of these wonders are under threat from climate change, pollution, and overtourism. The Great Barrier Reef in Australia, for example, has experienced severe coral bleaching due to rising ocean temperatures. Protecting these natural treasures requires global cooperation and sustainable practices. By reducing our carbon footprint and supporting conservation efforts, we can help preserve these wonders for future generations to enjoy.

---

What is the Great Barrier Reef experiencing according to the passage?
questions.solution: 文章说 The Great Barrier Reef has experienced severe coral bleaching due to rising ocean temperatures。

questions.title: The Evolution of Language
questions.content:
[Passage 17]

The Evolution of Language

Language is one of the most remarkable human abilities. It allows us to communicate complex ideas, share knowledge, and build relationships. Linguists estimate that there are approximately 7,000 languages spoken in the world today, though many are at risk of extinction. Languages evolve over time, influenced by factors such as migration, trade, and technological change. English, for example, has borrowed words from numerous languages including French, Latin, and Greek. The rise of the internet has accelerated language change, introducing new words and expressions like "selfie," "emoji," and "googling." While some worry that this rapid change is degrading language, others see it as a natural and enriching process that reflects the dynamic nature of human communication.

---

Approximately how many languages are spoken in the world today?
questions.solution: 文章说 there are approximately 7,000 languages spoken in the world today。

questions.title: The Journey of Entrepreneurship
questions.content:
[Passage 18]

The Journey of Entrepreneurship

Starting a business is both exciting and challenging. Entrepreneurs are individuals who identify opportunities and take risks to create new products or services. Successful entrepreneurs share certain traits: they are resilient, creative, and willing to learn from failure. Many well-known companies, including Apple and Amazon, started in garages or dorm rooms before growing into global enterprises. However, the path to success is rarely smooth. Most startups fail within the first few years, often due to lack of market demand, insufficient funding, or poor management. Despite these challenges, entrepreneurship remains a vital driver of economic growth and innovation. Governments and organizations around the world are increasingly supporting entrepreneurs through funding, mentorship programs, and business incubators.

---

What traits do successful entrepreneurs share?
questions.solution: 文章说 Successful entrepreneurs share certain traits: they are resilient, creative, and willing to learn from failure。
options: A. Laziness and fear of risk
B. Resilience, creativity, and willingness to learn
C. Desire for a simple life

questions.title: The Art of Communication
questions.content:
[Passage 19]

The Art of Communication

Effective communication is the cornerstone of healthy relationships, successful careers, and harmonious communities. It involves not just speaking clearly, but also listening actively and empathetically. Active listening means paying full attention to the speaker, asking clarifying questions, and providing feedback that shows understanding. Non-verbal communication, such as body language, facial expressions, and tone of voice, often conveys more than words alone. In fact, studies suggest that non-verbal cues account for a significant portion of the message in face-to-face communication. Cultural differences can also affect communication styles. What is considered polite in one culture may be seen as rude in another. Developing strong communication skills requires practice, self-awareness, and a genuine desire to understand others.

---

What does active listening involve?
questions.solution: 文章说 Active listening means paying full attention to the speaker, asking clarifying questions, and providing feedback。
options: B. Paying full attention and asking clarifying questions
C. Thinking about what to say next

questions.title: The Promise of Renewable Energy
questions.content:
[Passage 20]

The Promise of Renewable Energy

Renewable energy is energy that comes from sources that are naturally replenished, such as sunlight, wind, rain, and geothermal heat. Unlike fossil fuels, which are finite and contribute to climate change, renewable energy sources produce little to no greenhouse gas emissions. The cost of renewable energy technologies has dropped dramatically in recent years, making them increasingly competitive with traditional energy sources. Solar panels, for instance, are now more affordable than ever, and wind farms are being built in many parts of the world. However, challenges remain, including the need for better energy storage solutions and the upgrading of existing power grids. Despite these challenges, the transition to renewable energy is seen as essential for a sustainable future.

---

What are examples of renewable energy sources?
questions.solution: 文章说 Renewable energy is energy that comes from sources that are naturally replenished, such as sunlight, wind, rain, and geothermal heat。

questions.title: The Value of Lifelong Learning
questions.content:
[Passage 21]

The Value of Lifelong Learning

Lifelong learning is the ongoing, voluntary pursuit of knowledge for personal or professional reasons. In today's rapidly changing world, the skills and knowledge that were relevant a decade ago may no longer be sufficient. Technology, in particular, is evolving at an unprecedented pace, requiring workers to continuously update their skills. Lifelong learning can take many forms, from formal education like university courses to informal methods like reading books, attending workshops, or learning from online tutorials. The benefits extend beyond career advancement. Studies show that lifelong learning can improve cognitive function, boost self-confidence, and contribute to a sense of fulfillment. The most successful people are often those who maintain a curious and open mind throughout their lives.

---

What is lifelong learning?
questions.solution: 文章说 Lifelong learning is the ongoing, voluntary pursuit of knowledge。
options: B. The ongoing pursuit of knowledge
C. Learning only for a degree

questions.title: The Impact of Globalization
questions.content:
[Passage 22]

The Impact of Globalization

Globalization refers to the increasing interconnectedness of the world through trade, communication, and cultural exchange. It has transformed the global economy by allowing companies to operate across borders and consumers to access products from around the world. On the positive side, globalization has lifted millions of people out of poverty by creating jobs and fostering economic growth in developing countries. It has also facilitated the spread of ideas, technology, and cultural understanding. However, globalization has its critics. Some argue that it has led to job losses in developed countries, increased inequality, and the erosion of local cultures. The challenge for policymakers is to maximize the benefits of globalization while minimizing its negative effects through fair trade policies, worker protections, and cultural preservation efforts.

---

What is globalization?
questions.solution: 文章说 Globalization refers to the increasing interconnectedness of the world。
options: B. Increasing interconnectedness of the world

Some argue that it has led to job losses in developed countries, increased inequality, and the erosion of local cultures. The challenge for policymakers is to maximize the benefits of globalization while minimizing its negative effects through fair trade policies, worker protections, and cultural preservation efforts.

---

questions.solution: 文章说 globalization has lifted millions of people out of poverty。
options: B. Lifting millions out of poverty

questions.title: The Wonders of the Human Brain
questions.content:
[Passage 23]

The Wonders of the Human Brain

The human brain is arguably the most complex organ in the known universe. Weighing about three pounds, it contains roughly 86 billion neurons that communicate through trillions of connections called synapses. The brain is responsible for everything from basic bodily functions like breathing to complex cognitive processes like reasoning, creativity, and emotion. Despite decades of research, scientists are still uncovering the mysteries of how the brain works. Recent advances in neuroimaging technology have allowed researchers to observe the brain in action, leading to new insights about learning, memory, and mental health. Understanding the brain better could lead to breakthroughs in treating neurological disorders such as Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, and depression.

---

About how many neurons does the human brain contain?
questions.solution: 文章说 it contains roughly 86 billion neurons。

questions.title: The Ethics of Genetic Engineering
questions.content:
[Passage 24]

The Ethics of Genetic Engineering

Genetic engineering is a powerful technology that allows scientists to modify the DNA of living organisms. This technology has the potential to cure genetic diseases, improve crop yields, and even bring extinct species back to life. In medicine, gene therapy is being developed to treat conditions like cystic fibrosis and certain types of cancer. In agriculture, genetically modified crops can resist pests and tolerate harsh weather conditions, potentially reducing world hunger. However, genetic engineering raises significant ethical concerns. Critics worry about the unintended consequences of modifying organisms, the potential for creating "designer babies," and the impact on biodiversity. The debate over genetic engineering highlights the need for careful regulation and public discussion about how this technology should be used.

---

What can genetic engineering potentially do?
questions.solution: 文章说 This technology has the potential to cure genetic diseases, improve crop yields。
options: B. Cure genetic diseases and improve crop yields

questions.title: The Power of Music
questions.content:
[Passage 25]

The Power of Music

Music has been a part of human culture for thousands of years. It has the unique ability to evoke emotions, bring people together, and even heal. Research has shown that listening to music can reduce stress, lower blood pressure, and improve mood. Music therapy is used to help patients with conditions ranging from depression to Alzheimer's disease. Learning to play a musical instrument has been linked to improved cognitive abilities, including better memory and problem-solving skills. In many cultures, music plays a central role in rituals, celebrations, and storytelling. Whether it is a symphony orchestra, a rock band, or a simple folk song, music speaks to something deep within the human spirit. It transcends language barriers and connects people across different backgrounds and experiences.

---

What are the health benefits of listening to music mentioned in the passage?
questions.solution: 文章说 listening to music can reduce stress, lower blood pressure, and improve mood。
options: B. Reduced stress and lower blood pressure

questions.title: The Future of Transportation
questions.content:
[Passage 26]

The Future of Transportation

The way we move from place to place is undergoing a dramatic transformation. Electric vehicles are becoming more common as battery technology improves and charging infrastructure expands. Self-driving cars, once a science fiction dream, are now being tested on public roads in several countries. In urban areas, bike-sharing programs and improved public transit systems are reducing reliance on personal cars. Meanwhile, companies are developing hyperloop systems that could transport passengers at speeds of over 700 miles per hour through low-pressure tubes. These innovations promise to make transportation faster, cleaner, and more efficient. However, they also pose challenges, including the need for new regulations, infrastructure investment, and addressing concerns about job losses in traditional transportation industries.

---

What is becoming more common in transportation?
questions.solution: 文章说 Electric vehicles are becoming more common。

questions.title: The Art of Public Speaking
questions.content:
[Passage 27]

The Art of Public Speaking

Public speaking is one of the most common fears, yet it is also one of the most valuable skills in professional and personal life. The ability to communicate ideas clearly and persuasively to an audience can open doors to leadership opportunities and career advancement. Effective public speakers share several key practices: they know their audience, structure their message clearly, use stories and examples to illustrate points, and practice extensively. Managing nervousness is also crucial. Techniques such as deep breathing, visualization, and focusing on the message rather than oneself can help reduce anxiety. Like any skill, public speaking improves with practice. Joining organizations like Toastmasters or simply volunteering to present at meetings can provide valuable experience and feedback.

---

What is one of the most common fears mentioned?
questions.solution: 文章说 Public speaking is one of the most common fears。

questions.title: The Impact of Fast Fashion
questions.content:
[Passage 28]

The Impact of Fast Fashion

Fast fashion refers to the rapid production of inexpensive clothing to meet the latest trends. While it makes fashion affordable for consumers, it comes at a significant environmental and social cost. The fashion industry is one of the largest polluters in the world, responsible for about 10% of global carbon emissions and a major source of water pollution. The production of cheap clothing often relies on low-wage workers in developing countries who work in poor conditions. Additionally, the fast fashion model encourages a throwaway culture, where clothes are worn only a few times before being discarded. In response, a growing movement toward sustainable fashion is encouraging consumers to buy fewer, higher-quality items, support ethical brands, and recycle or donate old clothing.

---

What is fast fashion?
questions.solution: 文章说 Fast fashion refers to the rapid production of inexpensive clothing to meet the latest trends。
options: B. Rapid production of inexpensive clothing

questions.title: The Importance of Critical Thinking
questions.content:
[Passage 29]

The Importance of Critical Thinking

Critical thinking is the ability to analyze information objectively and make reasoned judgments. In an era of information overload and fake news, critical thinking skills are more important than ever. A critical thinker does not simply accept information at face value but questions assumptions, evaluates evidence, and considers alternative perspectives before reaching a conclusion. This skill is valuable in all areas of life, from making financial decisions to evaluating political claims and scientific research. Educational systems around the world are increasingly recognizing the importance of teaching critical thinking alongside traditional academic subjects. By developing this skill, individuals become better equipped to navigate the complexities of modern life and make informed decisions that affect themselves and their communities.

---

What is critical thinking?
questions.solution: 文章说 Critical thinking is the ability to analyze information objectively and make reasoned judgments。
options: B. Analyzing information objectively and making reasoned judgments

questions.title: The Rise of Remote Work
questions.content:
[Passage 30]

The Rise of Remote Work

Remote work, once a rare perk, has become mainstream in recent years, accelerated by the global pandemic. Technology such as video conferencing, cloud computing, and project management software has made it possible for many jobs to be done from anywhere with an internet connection. The benefits of remote work include greater flexibility, reduced commuting time, and access to a wider talent pool for employers. Many workers report higher job satisfaction and better work-life balance when working remotely. However, remote work also presents challenges. Some people struggle with isolation, difficulty separating work from personal life, and communication barriers with colleagues. As companies adapt to this new reality, hybrid models that combine remote and in-office work are becoming increasingly popular.

---

What has made remote work possible?
questions.solution: 文章说 Technology such as video conferencing, cloud computing, and project management software has made it possible。
options: B. Video conferencing and cloud computing

questions.title: The Ocean and Marine Life
questions.content:
[Passage 31]

The Ocean and Marine Life

The ocean covers more than 70% of the Earth's surface and is home to an incredible diversity of life. From microscopic plankton to massive blue whales, marine ecosystems are complex and interconnected. The ocean plays a crucial role in regulating the Earth's climate by absorbing carbon dioxide and heat. It also provides food and livelihoods for billions of people around the world. However, human activities are threatening marine life in numerous ways. Overfishing has depleted many fish populations, plastic pollution is harming marine animals, and climate change is causing ocean acidification and coral bleaching. Protecting the ocean requires international cooperation, sustainable fishing practices, and reducing plastic waste. Marine protected areas, where fishing and other activities are restricted, have proven effective in helping ecosystems recover.

---

How much of the Earth's surface does the ocean cover?
questions.solution: 文章说 The ocean covers more than 70% of the Earth's surface。

questions.title: The Philosophy of Happiness
questions.content:
[Passage 32]

The Philosophy of Happiness

Throughout history, philosophers have debated what it means to live a good life. Aristotle believed that happiness, or eudaimonia, comes from living virtuously and fulfilling one's potential. The Stoics argued that true happiness is found in accepting what we cannot control and focusing on our own thoughts and actions. In Eastern philosophy, Buddhism teaches that happiness comes from letting go of desires and attachments. Modern psychology has added to this conversation with research on positive emotions, resilience, and the importance of meaning and purpose. While different traditions offer different paths to happiness, they share a common insight: lasting happiness is not found in external possessions or achievements, but in how we relate to ourselves, others, and the world around us.

---

What did Aristotle believe about happiness?
questions.solution: 文章说 Aristotle believed that happiness comes from living virtuously and fulfilling one's potential。
options: B. It comes from living virtuously and fulfilling one's potential
C. It is impossible to achieve

questions.title: The Growth of E-commerce
questions.content:
[Passage 33]

The Growth of E-commerce

E-commerce, or electronic commerce, has transformed the way people shop. With just a few clicks, consumers can purchase almost anything online and have it delivered to their doorstep. Companies like Amazon, Alibaba, and Shopify have revolutionized retail by creating platforms that connect buyers and sellers around the world. The convenience of online shopping, combined with competitive pricing and vast product selection, has driven the rapid growth of e-commerce. During the COVID-19 pandemic, online shopping surged as people stayed home and avoided physical stores. While e-commerce offers many benefits, it also poses challenges for traditional brick-and-mortar stores, many of which have struggled to compete. The future of retail is likely to be a blend of online and offline experiences, with technology enhancing the shopping experience in both realms.

---

What is e-commerce?
questions.solution: 文章说 E-commerce, or electronic commerce, has transformed the way people shop online。
options: A. Shopping only in physical stores
B. Electronic commerce or online shopping

The convenience of online shopping, combined with competitive pricing and vast product selection, has driven the rapid growth of e-commerce. During the COVID-19 pandemic, online shopping surged as people stayed home and avoided physical stores. While e-commerce offers many benefits, it also poses challenges for traditional brick-and-mortar stores, many of which have struggled to compete. The future of retail is likely to be a blend of online and offline experiences, with technology enhancing the shopping experience in both realms.

---

What drove the rapid growth of e-commerce?
questions.solution: 文章说 The convenience of online shopping, combined with competitive pricing and vast product selection, has driven the rapid growth。
options: B. Convenience, competitive pricing, and vast selection

questions.title: The Science of Memory
questions.content:
[Passage 34]

The Science of Memory

Memory is one of the most fascinating functions of the human brain. It allows us to store and retrieve information, from simple facts to complex life experiences. Scientists generally divide memory into three types: sensory memory, which lasts only a few seconds; short-term memory, which can hold a limited amount of information for about 20-30 seconds; and long-term memory, which can store vast amounts of information for years or even a lifetime. The process of converting short-term memories into long-term ones is called consolidation, and it often occurs during sleep. Techniques like spaced repetition, where information is reviewed at increasing intervals, have been shown to significantly improve long-term retention. Understanding how memory works can help students learn more effectively and may lead to better treatments for memory-related disorders.

---

How many types of memory are mentioned?
questions.solution: 文章提到三种记忆类型：sensory memory, short-term memory, and long-term memory。

questions.title: The Value of Failure
questions.content:
[Passage 35]

The Value of Failure

Failure is often seen as something to be avoided at all costs. However, many of the world's most successful people view failure differently. They see it as an essential part of the learning process. Thomas Edison famously said that he had not failed 10,000 times in inventing the light bulb but had found 10,000 ways that did not work. Failure provides valuable feedback about what does not work and can lead to new insights and approaches. It also builds resilience and character. The key is not to avoid failure but to learn from it and keep moving forward. In education and business, there is a growing recognition that creating environments where people feel safe to take risks and fail is essential for fostering innovation and growth.

---

How do many successful people view failure?
questions.solution: 文章说 many of the world's most successful people... see it as an essential part of the learning process。
options: B. As an essential part of learning
C. As the end of everything
D. As a sign of weakness

questions.title: The Benefits of Meditation
questions.content:
[Passage 36]

The Benefits of Meditation

Meditation is an ancient practice that has gained widespread popularity in recent years. It involves training the mind to focus and redirect thoughts, often through techniques such as mindfulness or concentration on the breath. Scientific research has shown that regular meditation can reduce stress, improve attention, and increase emotional well-being. Studies have found that meditation can actually change the structure of the brain, increasing gray matter in areas associated with learning, memory, and emotional regulation. Even just a few minutes of daily meditation can make a difference. Many companies now offer meditation programs for employees, and schools are introducing mindfulness practices to help students manage stress and improve focus. In a world full of distractions, meditation offers a way to find calm and clarity.

---

What is meditation?
questions.solution: 文章说 It involves training the mind to focus and redirect thoughts。
options: B. Training the mind to focus and redirect thoughts

questions.title: The Digital Divide
questions.content:
[Passage 37]

The Digital Divide

The digital divide refers to the gap between those who have access to modern information and communication technology and those who do not. This divide exists not only between developed and developing countries but also within countries, between urban and rural areas, and across different socioeconomic groups. Lack of access to the internet and digital devices can limit educational opportunities, job prospects, and access to essential services like healthcare and government information. During the COVID-19 pandemic, the digital divide became particularly apparent as students without reliable internet access struggled to participate in online learning. Bridging this divide requires investment in infrastructure, affordable devices, and digital literacy programs. Ensuring that everyone has access to digital technology is essential for creating a more equitable society.

---

What is the digital divide?
questions.solution: 文章说 The digital divide refers to the gap between those who have access to modern information and communication technology and those who do not。
options: B. The gap between those with and without access to technology
